commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces recursive media-store `Error.cause` traversal with a bounded iterative walk and adds regression coverage for nested `ENOSPC` and cyclic cause chains.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +104. Total +118 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main recursively follows `err.cause` without a cycle or depth guard inside the media write retry path. I did not execute the repro in this read-only review, but a cyclic cause thrown from file-store write is enough to make the current helper recurse until stack overflow.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining gate is contributor-provided real behavior proof; there is no narrow code repair for automation to perform.

target.title: Guard media store error cause traversal
target.body:
## Summary

- replace recursive media store error cause traversal with an iterative bounded walk
- guard against cyclic `Error.cause` chains and throwing cause getters
- add media store regression coverage for nested `ENOSPC` preservation and cyclic cause chains

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/media/store.ts src/media/store.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.media.config.ts src/media/store.test.ts`

## Context

The previous recursive `findErrorWithCode(err, code)` could overflow the JS stack if a lower-level filesystem/file-store error had a very deep or cyclic `cause` chain. The new implementation keeps the existing errno lookup semantics while making the traversal bounded and cycle-safe.
target.author: ihyz
